evidence: pure indulgence in bad Sci-Fi and fantasy with no particular solid foundation.
I absolutely love this movie! As a child of the 80's and 90's I saw my childhood flash by in this movie.
There's nothing too complicated to comprehend, just one scene poorly connected to the next.
The story itself is pretty good, Eddie Murphy does a great job of portraying a distressed character.
The characters were incredibly well developed, but still true to their original 'programmed' purpose.
I left this movie wanting more.
it didn't have a huge budget, but the director made up for the lack of budget with quality work and an artistic approach.
The acting was great, if Tron Legacy didn't do it for me already now I really have the hots for Olivia Wilde.
Dreamworks generally doesn't make bad cartoons as far as I can tell.
I don't care if you hated the original Planet of the Apes movie series.
I consider this a genuine nerd flick, not because it's nerdy, but because it portrays nerds with all their shortfalls in a positive light.
The humor was very intelligent - only Kevin Smith rivals this movies ability to make proper use of a running gags.
I left this movie wanting more.
I can't emphasize enough how much this movie portrays the main character Flint Lockwood as a genuine realistic geek/nerd/dork.

summary: The user enjoys a variety of genres, particularly sci-fi and family-oriented films, often appreciating simple narratives with fast pacing. They value emotional resonance and character development, while showing moderate sensitivity to production quality and low brand loyalty.
